Title: Wheat glutamine synthetase TaGSr-4B is a candidate gene for a QTL of thousand grain weight on chromosome 4B

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glutamine synthetase, cytosolic GS1 subtype TaGSr
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Nitrogen metabolism; alanine, aspartate and glutamate metabolism; arginine biosynthesis; glyoxylate and dicarboxylate metabolism
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies nitrogen assimilate pool and promotes remobilization that supports grain filling and grain weight
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; supported as major candidate gene underlying the TGW QTL
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol; phloem sieve elements (TaGSr subgroup reported in discussion)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Grain protein/nitrogen assimilate pool associated with thousand grain weight
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports grain filling and yield formation through nitrogen remobilization coordinated with carbon remobilization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Favorable Westonia allele is associated with increased thousand grain weight by 5-12% and TaGSr-4B is the most likely candidate gene for the 4B TGW QTL
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL fine mapping with 90K SNPs; RNA-seq differential expression during grain filling; gene sequencing; CAP marker development from promoter SNP; QTL reanalysis with gene marker; allele-phenotype association across environments
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression gradually increases during grain filling; strongly enhanced under drought, especially in Westonia; TaGSr reported previously as highly expressed in peduncle
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Stem/main stem during 11-31 days after anthesis; increasing through grain filling; higher under drought at 31 DAA, especially in Westonia
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought versus well-watered during grain filling; multiple field environments; nitrogen levels 0, 50, 100 kg/ha
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter polymorphism analyzed; no transgenic promoter used
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of Westonia and Kauz stems during grain filling under drought and irrigated conditions; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No clear penalty reported; possible linkage/combined contribution with plant height QTL on 4B cannot be completely excluded
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A CAP marker (GS_4B) based on promoter SNP is ready for marker-assisted breeding to improve thousand grain weight; favorable Westonia allele is a useful target for selection
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TaGSr-4B is identical to TaGSr1 and distinct from TaGSr-4A and TaGSr-4D. Coding region differences were not found between Westonia and Kauz; polymorphisms occur in promoter, intron 1 and 3' UTR. Two transcripts were observed in Kauz but one in Westonia. Promoter polymorphism includes water-stress-related motif in Kauz and GA-responsive motif in Westonia.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaGSr-4B (TraesCS4B02G240900) was prioritized from 773 genes in the 4B TGW QTL cluster using grain-filling RNA-seq and transcript-pattern differences. 2. Its expression increased during grain filling and was enhanced by drought, especially in Westonia, consistent with a role in nitrogen remobilization. 3. A promoter SNP-derived CAP marker mapped TaGSr-4B to the TGW QTL region, and in four of seven environments the TGW QTL localized on the TaGSr-4B locus. 4. Westonia versus Kauz alleles showed significant TGW differences in eight environments, with the Westonia allele increasing TGW by 5-12%. 5. The paper concludes TaGSr-4B is the most likely candidate gene for the 4B thousand grain weight QTL and useful for breeding.
